source_title: What People Think About Green Smart Homes
article_summary: This paper reports two online surveys (general end users and experienced amateur automators) investigating attitudes, practices, and needs about smart-home automations with a focus on environmental sustainability; respondents show clear interest in monitoring energy consumption, receiving recommendations for optimal appliance activation times, and tools to identify excessive consumption. Experienced users report concrete energy-focused automations: HVAC scheduling tied to occupancy and windows, lighting and appliance shutoff based on presence, load management to avoid meter overload, and synchronizing device operation with photovoltaic production; both groups request better UX, integration/standardization, and AI-assisted suggestions to support greener behavior. The authors conclude that sustainable-management features (monitoring, recommendation, conflict detection) and improved end-user development interfaces are needed to make green automations accessible to more users.
